sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Back pain
History of Present Illness
The patient reports back pain, which he describes as shifting and has been present for the last four days. Initially, the pain was intermittent, but now it's constant. He reports that the pain started on his right side and has moved to his left. He describes the pain as coming and going at first, but now it's been constant for the last 48 hours. He took Tylenol, but it didn't help much. He also reports feeling dizzy and lightheaded when exerting himself, such as climbing stairs, and experiences some abdominal pain. He has some muscle aches and reports chest pain. He has a past medical history of kidney stones and migraines. He's been diligent about taking his migraine medication (imitrex) and has been following his acid reflux diet. He's been ordering take-out and fast food to avoid going up and down the stairs.
Findings
Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation on the right-hand side and in the abdomen. The patient reports muscle aches. Blood pressure is slightly elevated. The creatinine level is slightly elevated. Examination reveals right-hand tenderness and abdominal tenderness.
Assessment
The doctor suspects a recurrence of kidney stones. The patient's symptoms and physical exam findings are most consistent with back pain.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: The doctor orders a CT scan without contrast of the abdomen and pelvis. The doctor orders Ultram 50 milligrams as needed every six hours for pain. The nurse will be in touch with you in a day or so to schedule the CT scan. The doctor orders a refill of protonix 40 milligrams daily. Follow up in four weeks if symptoms persist. Push fluids and strain urine.
medical_concepts: CT scan without contrast of the abdomen and pelvis
CT scan without contrast of the abdomen and pelvis
